title: Question: Asynchronous Reset
body:
---


Author Name: **Aki Niimura**
Original Redmine Message: 75 from https://www.veripool.org



---

Hello Verilator experts,

I have used Verilator (converted to C++) to simulate a design that uses two clocks (ACLK and HCLK).
The design contains a clock boundary crossing logic that requires flop with asynchronous reset.
(See the code snippet below)

When I run simulation using the generated C++ code, the logic was behaving incorrectly.
To overcome the problems, I did the following:
1. tied two clocks together (ACLK = HCLK)
1. skipped the clock boundary logic (using `ifdef verilator to switch)

Now, the simulation is delivering the results I'm expecting.

My question to Verilator experts are:
1. Is what I did appropriate?
1. Is there any common rule to follow when it comes asynchronous logic?
1. Is there any good coding style of async logic that can be tolerated by Verilator?

Any suggestions will be appreciated.

Best regards,
Aki Niimura

*[Code excerpt]*
```

always @(posedge ACLK)
     aImstart_q <= aImstart_d;

assign aImstart_d =
     ~ARESETn ? {3{1'b0}} :
         { aImstart_q[1:0], hImstart_q } ;

always @(posedge HCLK or posedge asynHrst_d)
     if(asynHrst_d)
         hImstart_q <= 1'b0;
     else
         hImstart_q <= hImstart_d;

assign hImstart_d =
     HIMSTARTP ? 1'b1 :
         hImstart_q ;

assign asynHrst_d = |{
     ~HRESETn,
     aImstart_q[1]
     };


```
